data.title: [fix-bug skill]: orchestrator invokes Skill("review") -> built-in PR-review skill loads instead of dso review flow
data.description:
## Incident Overview

When running /dso:fix-bug, the orchestrator reached the commit-workflow step and tried to invoke the DSO code-review flow via the Skill tool. The DSO project does not expose a '/dso:review' skill — instead, /dso:commit internally invokes REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md. In practice, the CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table lists '/dso:review' as 'Code review via sub-agent', which creates an implicit expectation that it exists.

## Observed Behavior

- Orchestrator called Skill(skill='review') from within /dso:fix-bug commit flow
- The built-in Claude Code /review skill loaded (PR-review for GitHub: runs 'gh pr list', 'gh pr view <number>', 'gh pr diff <number>')
- The built-in /review is the WRONG flow for an in-progress local commit; it expects a PR number and operates on remote state
- No warning or redirect to the DSO code-review workflow was emitted

## Expected Behavior

Either:
(a) '/dso:review' should exist as a first-class skill that wraps REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and dispatches dso:code-reviewer-* sub-agents, OR
(b) CLAUDE.md Quick Reference and fix-bug/commit skill guidance should be updated to explicitly state 'review happens inline via REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md — no Skill tool call', so orchestrators read the workflow file instead of reaching for Skill('review').

## Actual Impact

Orchestrator loses context attempting to use the built-in skill, then has to fall back to reading REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and dispatching reviewers manually. Wastes tokens and risks the orchestrator silently using the built-in PR-review flow on a local change (producing a review of wrong state).

## Reproduce

1. Start /dso:fix-bug on any bug where a code fix is applied
2. Reach the commit-workflow step that requires review
3. Orchestrator invokes Skill(skill='review')
4. The built-in /review skill loads (PR number: prompt) instead of the DSO review flow

## Root Cause Hypothesis (pre-investigation)

CLAUDE.md Quick Reference at row 'Code review via sub-agent | /dso:review | /dso:plan-review' implies a /dso:review skill exists. The DSO plugin registers many dso:* skills but no dso:review. The Skill tool fuzzy-matches 'review' to the built-in review skill (not the DSO namespace). This creates a silent routing failure where the orchestrator believes it has invoked the DSO flow but has actually invoked the built-in PR-review flow.
